themes.summary: Micron ($MU) is poised for strong earnings, continuing significant beats driven by AI buildouts and hyperscaler capex, with the memory supercycle intact through 2026-27. China's CXMT is also projected for massive revenue growth, though some analysts warn of potential memory price corrections by H2 2027.

The KOSPI experienced a significant -9.99% sell-off, its fifth worst day ever, attributed to MSCI's EM classification, retail deleveraging, and profit-taking. This triggered a broad semiconductor market drop, with SK Hynix and Samsung down over 12%, extending to US names like $MU and $SNDK.

Tech investment as a % of GDP now exceeds dot-com bubble levels, with hyperscaler capex for 2026 up 80%. Concerns are rising about a potential AI stock frenzy bubble in Taiwan, evidenced by a 100%+ market surge, high margin debt, and increased trading defaults.

The AI sector is seeing agent systems enter a self-iteration phase, driven by new Agent Scaling Laws and synthetic-data flywheels, with software engineering as the first vertical for AGI-level automation. Commercialization is shifting towards ROI discipline, with Anthropic and OpenAI leading model-layer growth, and compute remaining a core constraint across the AI value chain.

TSMC plans significant investment in a new advanced chip packaging plant for CoWoS-L in Central Taiwan, potentially retrofitting an AUO plant for faster deployment. The company is also reportedly pushing for a 5-10% price increase across all advanced nodes, including 7nm, following trends seen in memory pricing.

Vertiv ($VRT) is a direct beneficiary of the AI infrastructure buildout, providing critical thermal management and power architecture as rack power density increases and liquid cooling becomes mandatory. Dell ($DELL) is also leveraging its operational scale to integrate complex, liquid-cooled GPU systems globally.

Astera Labs ($ALAB) is transitioning to an AI fabric platform provider, addressing data bottlenecks with PCIe Gen6, CXL, and optical interconnects. MediaTek secured Google's TPU v9 chip orders with a 336G SerDes solution after Broadcom's 448G solution faced production delays. Cerebras Systems ($CBRS) reported solid Q2 and FY revenue guidance but saw a stock drop.

Market participants are reacting to "BS narratives" like predictions of 2-3 rate hikes without new macroeconomic data, leading to dip-buying opportunities in semis. There's also a rotation of profits from bottleneck trades into the space sector and cash reserves.

Starlink is expanding services to airlines, while discussions are emerging around orbital data centers as terrestrial data centers face constraints. Lower launch costs make large orbital infrastructure more feasible, though GW-scale commercialization remains challenging.

Advantest and OpenLight partnered to develop scalable, high-volume manufacturing (HVM) test solutions for silicon photonics (SiPh) devices, addressing the bottleneck in testing electro-optical (EO) devices like the Optical Engine for AI/HPC workloads.
The ongoing debate between copper and optical interconnects for AI infrastructure is highlighted, emphasizing the critical choices in high-speed data transmission for data centers.
STMicroelectronics ($STM) has informed clients of a new round of price hikes for microcontrollers (MCUs) starting June 28, leading to increased share prices for Taiwanese MCU manufacturers.

Sivers lasers are potentially powering robotics and physical AI applications, including Boston Dynamics Atlas via LG Innotek and within the Nvidia Hyperion ecosystem, with Apple possibly using Sivers' optical sensing modules for wearables.
Alphabet ($GOOGL) is set to replace Verizon ($VZ) in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, reflecting a shift in the composition of the benchmark index.
